signals.text: Our testing identified three significant deficiencies in the procurement process.
signals.rationale: Authors conclude from testing that three deficiencies exist — a direct observational finding.

signals.text: in 18 of 90 sampled transactions (20%), required market research documentation was either missing or inadequate to support the contracting officer's price determination. This represents a material weakness in the office's compliance with FAR Part 10.
signals.rationale: Authors report a measured rate of missing/inadequate documentation and conclude it is a material weakness.

signals.text: Of 12 sole-source awards reviewed, four lacked complete J&A documentation, and an additional two contained J&A documents that did not address all required elements.
signals.rationale: Authors report specific counts of non-compliant contract files identified during review.

signals.text: In 7 transactions, the same individual served as both the requisitioner and the contracting officer's technical representative, creating a control gap that elevates the risk of undetected error or fraud.
signals.rationale: Authors identify a specific segregation-of-duties failure and its risk implication from testing.

signals.text: Recommendation 1: Issue updated guidance to all contracting officers reinforcing the FAR Part 10 market research documentation requirements, with specific examples of acceptable documentation.
signals.rationale: Explicit recommendation for a corrective action directed at the Administrator.

signals.text: Recommendation 2: Establish a quarterly file review process for all sole-source awards over $1 million to verify J&A completeness before contract award.
signals.rationale: Explicit recommendation to create a new oversight process for sole-source awards.

signals.text: Recommendation 3: Update the office's procurement standard operating procedure to formally prohibit the dual role of requisitioner and COTR on the same transaction.
signals.rationale: Explicit recommendation to revise SOPs to address the segregation-of-duties gap.

signals.text: The Administrator may also wish to consider implementing a system-level control in the procurement system that flags such conflicts.
signals.rationale: Indirect recommendation suggesting an additional system-level control for the Administrator to consider.

signals.text: The Administrator concurred with all three recommendations.
signals.rationale: Formal management concurrence is a committed decision, not merely a suggestion.

signals.text: The office has committed to issuing updated market research guidance by March 31, 2025
signals.rationale: A specific, time-bound commitment to a corrective action has been made.

signals.text: has already initiated procurement of a contract management system upgrade that will support the dual-role control flagging described in Recommendation 3.
signals.rationale: Procurement has already been initiated — a definite action underway, not a suggestion.

signals.text: We will follow up on implementation status in our FY2025 review.
signals.rationale: OIG commits to a specific future follow-up action in the next review cycle.
